title: erica denticulata var. retusa
intro: Erica denticulata var. retusa, commonly known as the twisted heath, is a plant species in the heath family (Ericaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia, and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe respiratory issues and promote wound healing. Traditionally, Erica denticulata var. retusa is prepared as a tea or infusion, and has been used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
